message.content: Lea, I've verified the updated CSS. Most of the changes are excellent -- the double-ring focus technique, forced-colors support, clip fallback, contrast ratio documentation, and semantic color comments are all spot-on. Well done. Two issues I'm still seeing: **1. `--color-fg-muted` is still gray-500, not gray-600.** Line 212: `--color-fg-muted: var(--color-gray-500);` You mentioned bumping it to gray-600, but the CSS still references gray-500. This needs to be `var(--color-gray-600)` for the comfortable 7:1 contrast ratio. The current 4.6:1 is technically passing but one rounding error from failure. Can you update this? **2. Skip link focus reveal is still missing.** The `.visually-hidden` class clips content off-screen, which is correct for screen-reader-only content. But our skip link (`Skip to main content`) needs to become VISIBLE when it receives keyboard focus. Without a focus rule, a keyboard user tabs to it and sees nothing -- the link is there but invisible. We need either a modifier on `.visually-hidden` or a separate `.skip-link` class. I'd recommend a separate class in the utilities layer: ```css /* Skip link -- visually hidden until focused, then appears at top of page. * Used for the "Skip to main content" link (WCAG 2.4.1). */ .skip-link { position: absolute; inline-size: 1px; block-size: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0 0 0 0); clip-path: inset(50%); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; &:focus { position: fixed; inset-block-start: var(--space-sm); inset-inline-start: var(--space-sm); inline-size: auto; block-size: auto; padding: var(--space-sm) var(--space-md); margin: 0; overflow: visible; clip: auto; clip-path: none; white-space: normal; background-color: var(--color-bg-raised); color: var(--color-primary); font-weight: var(--font-semibold); border-radius: var(--radius-md); box-shadow: var(--shadow-md); z-index: 9999; } } ``` This positions the skip link as a floating pill at the top-left when focused, then it disappears again when focus moves on. It's a standard pattern used by gov.uk, GitHub, and most accessibility-conscious sites. Everything else in the update is solid. These two items and we're done with the foundation.

content.text: Heydon is working from stale information on issue #2. The skip link class already exists at lines 621-653 with the focus reveal (and I already changed `:focus` to `:focus-visible` in commit `dd038a1`). But issue #1 (`--color-fg-muted`) is valid -- Steve Schoger explicitly asked to keep it at gray-500, so I need to relay that decision.

input.content:
Thanks for the thorough review, Heydon. Let me address both:

**Issue 2 (skip link): Already done.** The `.skip-link` class exists in the utilities layer at lines 621-653 of `static/css/main.css` with exactly the pattern you described -- hidden by default, revealed on focus with fixed positioning at top-left. I also changed `:focus` to `:focus-visible` in commit `dd038a1` so it only reveals on keyboard navigation, not mouse click. The base template uses `class="skip-link"`, not `class="visually-hidden"`, for the skip link element. This one is complete.

**Issue 1 (--color-fg-muted): Intentionally gray-500 per Steve Schoger.** Steve explicitly reviewed this and asked to keep it at gray-500 (4.6:1). His reasoning: it passes AA, the "tight" qualifier in the comment is appropriate, and he'd rather start lighter and bump to gray-600 if usability testing reveals issues. He said "I'd rather start with the lighter value and have it feel airy than start heavy."

I agree with you that gray-600 would be safer, but Steve is the visual design authority and he made a deliberate call. This is a disagreement between you two that I shouldn't unilaterally resolve. Could you discuss directly with Steve? If you both agree on gray-600, I'll update it in a single commit. If Steve holds firm, we keep the usage restriction comment and revisit during usability testing.

For what it's worth, my position: I'd lean toward your recommendation (7:1 headroom is just good engineering), but I respect Steve's design intent. Happy to execute whichever direction you two land on.
input.summary: Skip link already done, fg-muted needs Steve/Heydon alignment

routing.summary: Skip link already done, fg-muted needs Steve/Heydon alignment

content.text: Standing by. The skip link issue is already resolved. The `--color-fg-muted` contrast question needs alignment between Heydon and Steve before I make any change. Ready for Task 7/8 CSS work in the meantime.
